---
title: "Session 01"
subtitle: "Course Introduction, Data Science, and R Basics"
format: revealjs
---

## Introduction to Data Science {.cover-standard}

![](../_shared/assets/Logo_UCAB_1.png){width=170px}

Session 01  
Course Introduction, Data Science, and R Basics

Giorgio Cunto

## Main Message {.statement-slide .layout-band}

This first session has two goals:

- Introduce the course and its working structure
- Build the first operational workflow in `R` and `RStudio`

## Roadmap For Today {.agenda-slide}

- Course presentation
- What `Data Science` is
- `R` and `RStudio`
- Basic operations in `R`
- Data types, vectors, and indexing

## Course Structure {.layout-dark-panel}

The course is organized in four modules:

- `1`: Data Science And `R` Fundamentals
- `2`: Visualization And Exploration
- `3`: Analytical Methods And Machine Learning
- `4`: Reproducibility

## Evaluation Structure {.layout-dark-panel}

- `10` Problem Sets
- `4` Project Deliverables
- `1` Final Project Presentation
- Class Participation

## What Is Data Science? {.section-divider .layout-band}

Data science is the use of data, computation, and analytical reasoning to answer questions and support decisions.

In practice, it usually involves:

- Asking questions
- Organizing data
- Exploring patterns
- Communicating findings

## Why `R` And `RStudio`? {.layout-dark-panel}

- `R` is the language that executes code
- `RStudio` is the environment where we write and manage code
- Together they give us a reproducible workflow for the course

## `R` Is Not The Same As `RStudio`

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
### `R`

- Programming language
- Executes commands
- Stores objects
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### `RStudio`

- Development environment
- Helps organize scripts, console, files, and plots
- Makes the workflow easier to manage
:::
:::

## RStudio Pane Layout

RStudio gives us four practical spaces:

- `The Script Editor`
- `The Console`
- `The Environment`
- `The Files, Plots, Packages, And Help panes`

## Basic Operations In `R`

```{r}
5 + 2
5 - 2
2 ^ 2
sqrt(16)
abs(-10)
```

## Objects In `R`

```{r}
x <- 5
x

x <- 5 + 2
x

var_text <- "UCAB"
var_text
```

## Vectors

```{r}
vector_num <- c(5, 2, 10)
vector_num
mean(vector_num)
max(vector_num)
min(vector_num)
```

## Data Types

```{r}
a <- 2
class(a)

a <- "2"
class(a)

z <- 3 == 2
class(z)
```

## A First Data Example

```{r}
library(dslabs)
data(murders)

class(murders)
head(murders)
```

## Accessing Columns

```{r}
murders$population
pop <- murders$population
class(pop)
length(pop)
```

## Ordering And Indexing

```{r}
codes <- c("italy" = 380, "canada" = 124, "egypt" = 818)
codes[2]
codes[c(1, 3)]
codes["italy"]
```

## Practice Questions

- What class is `murders$state`?
- Which state has the largest population?
- Create a named vector with three countries and three codes

## Closing The Session

Today we established:

- The course structure
- The role of `R` and `RStudio`
- The first `R` objects and operations
- A first look at data structures and indexing

## Contact {.closing-standard}

Giorgio Cunto

`gcuntomo@ucab.edu.ve`

`linkedin.com/in/giorgiocmorales`
